description: Property types. Scope is Track, Object, or both. Endpoints MUST ignore unknown Property types, skipping them using the length field. Even type values use VarInt KVP encoding; odd type values use Bytes KVP encoding.

ranges.description: Registration policy for the Property Type space.

entries.policy: Standards Action or IESG Approval (1-byte encoding)
Reserved for application-specific use (1-byte encoding, no registration permitted)
Reserved for application-specific use (2-byte encoding, no registration permitted)
Reserved for Mandatory Track Properties (§2.5.1). Properties registered in this range MUST have Track scope; Object scope properties MUST NOT be registered in this range.

provisional.description: Provisional registrations for other active drafts in the moq working group. These share the same Property Type space as the entries above, so they are not a private namespace and must not collide with it. Both stacks carry ids from an older revision of draft-ietf-moq-loc.

entries.name: TIMESTAMP
entries.notes: Both stacks call this CaptureTimestamp and put it at 0x02, which is OBJECT_DELIVERY_TIMEOUT.

entries.name: VIDEO_FRAME_MARKING
entries.notes: Both stacks put this at 0x04, which is MAX_CACHE_DURATION.

entries.name: AUDIO_LEVEL
entries.notes: Both stacks put this at 0x06, which is SUBGROUP_DELIVERY_TIMEOUT and also TIMESTAMP in this table — a draft-18 peer decodes an audio level as a timestamp rather than failing.
